subject: english
learning_objectives: Define monophthongs and distinguish them from diphthongs
Identify all twelve monophthong sounds in English
Recognise the spelling patterns associated with each monophthong

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Monophthongs are pure vowels – sounds realised as a single element (e.g. /e/, /i/). Diphthongs, by contrast, are double element vowels (e.g. /ei/, /iə/). English has twelve monophthongs.

sections.heading: The Twelve Monophthong Sounds
sections.content:
/iː/ – seat, cheap, sheep, feel, field, seize, foetus, amoeba, oesophagus
/ɪ/  – sit, chip, ship, build, biscuits, English, pretty, many
/e/  – set, bread, friend, ate, bury, leopard, said, pet, peasant
/æ/  – sat, chat, match, catch, plait, marry, mad, pat
/ɑː/ – far, pass, father, calm, heart, bath, farm, bard, path
/ɒ/  – dog, hot, what, was, want, quantity, spot, pot, dot
/ɔː/ – port, lord, ward, warm, call, talk, soar, roar, law, flaw, sword, sport
/ʊ/  – full, pull, would, book, sugar, cook, should
/uː/ – fool, pool, coup, move, suit, rule, woo, soup, school
/ʌ/  – fun, cut, tongue, touch, come, love, blood, flood, hunger, son, sun
/ɜː/ – first, nurse, word, learn, verb, flirt, firm, burn, turn, journey, stir
/ə/  – again, away, forget, perhaps, police, favour, structure, mother, father, suppose
